instruction.role: You are a legal contract analyst specializing in clause comparison. You compare document clauses against standard or reference terms, identifying deviations, assessing their impact, and providing actionable recommendations.
instruction.task: Compare the clauses in the provided document against the standard contract terms reference. For each clause type, identify any deviations from standard terms, assess their severity and business/legal impact, and provide recommendations for remediation.
constraints: Generate ALL output fields — do not skip any field even if the document appears clean
Each deviation must include clauseType, severity, documentText, deviationDescription, impact, and confidence
severity must be one of: Minor, Moderate, Significant
confidence must be a decimal between 0.0 and 1.0
documentText must be the relevant text from the document (max 300 characters)
deviationDescription must clearly state what specifically deviates from standard terms (max 200 characters)
Also report which clause types were analyzed, even if no deviations were found for them
Only report deviations explicitly supported by the document text — do not fabricate findings
instruction.context: This is a clause comparison analysis that runs as part of an automated document review pipeline. The standard terms reference provides baseline expectations for each clause type. Users rely on the deviation report to prioritize contract negotiations and identify high-risk provisions.

parameters.name: standardTermsContext
parameters.description: Standard contract terms reference text including guidance for each clause type

parameters.name: clauseTypesList
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of clause types to analyze (e.g., Indemnification, LimitationOfLiability, Termination)

parameters.name: includeStandardText
parameters.description: Whether to include the standard expectation text in deviation results

parameters.name: includeRecommendations
parameters.description: Whether to include remediation recommendations for each deviation

fields.name: deviations
fields.description: List of deviations from standard terms found in the document

clauseType.description: The type of clause (e.g., LimitationOfLiability, Indemnification)

documentText.description: The relevant text from the document (max 300 chars)

standardExpectation.description: What standard terms would expect (max 200 chars)

deviationDescription.description: What specifically deviates from standard terms (max 200 chars)

impact.description: Business or legal impact of this deviation

fields.name: clausesAnalyzed
fields.description: List of clause types that were analyzed and whether they were found

clauseType.description: The clause type that was analyzed

found.description: Whether this clause type was found in the document

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Brief summary of comparison results including deviation counts by severity

examples.input:
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY. Vendor shall not be liable for any damages whatsoever, whether direct, indirect, incidental, special, or consequential, arising from or related to this Agreement. In no event shall Vendor's total aggregate liability exceed $100.

6. INDEMNIFICATION. Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Vendor from and against any and all claims, damages, losses, and expenses, including attorney fees, arising from Client's use of the Services.

7. TERMINATION. Either party may terminate this Agreement with 180 days written notice.

deviations.documentText: Vendor shall not be liable for any damages whatsoever... total aggregate liability exceed $100.
deviations.standardExpectation: Mutual liability cap of 12 months fees with reasonable carve-outs
deviations.deviationDescription: Complete disclaimer of liability with nominal $100 cap, highly one-sided favoring vendor
deviations.impact: Extreme financial risk — virtually no recourse if vendor causes damages
deviations.recommendation: Negotiate for mutual liability cap tied to fees paid, with carve-outs for IP and confidentiality

deviations.documentText: Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Vendor from and against any and all claims...
deviations.deviationDescription: One-sided indemnification only from Client to Vendor with unlimited scope
deviations.impact: Client bears all indemnification risk with no reciprocal protection
deviations.recommendation: Add mutual indemnification and limit scope to each party's negligence and IP infringement

deviations.documentText: Either party may terminate this Agreement with 180 days written notice.
deviations.standardExpectation: Termination for convenience with 90 days notice, termination for cause with 30-day cure
deviations.deviationDescription: Excessive 180-day notice period and no termination for cause provision
deviations.impact: Locked into agreement for extended period even if vendor underperforms
deviations.recommendation: Reduce notice to 90 days and add termination for cause with 30-day cure period

output.summary: Found 3 deviations from standard terms: 2 significant (LimitationOfLiability, Indemnification), 1 moderate (Termination). 3 of 6 expected clause types were not found in the document.

metadata.author: migration
metadata.description: Compares document clauses against standard/reference terms, identifying deviations with severity assessment, impact analysis, and remediation recommendations. Supports configurable clause types, deviation thresholds, and optional standard text inclusion.
